752	// TestExecuteBeadWorkerReviewBoundedByMaxTier verifies that REQUEST_CHANGES
753	// reopens the bead for the next attempt but does not cause an infinite loop
754	// within a single worker.Run call. The bead is visited once, the reviewer
755	// returns REQUEST_CHANGES, the bead is reopened — but the "attempted" map
756	// prevents it from being picked up again in the same run.
757	func TestExecuteBeadWorkerReviewBoundedByMaxTier(t *testing.T) {
758		// Use a single-bead store so we can assert "exactly one attempt".
759		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
760		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
761		only := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-bound-1", Title: "Impossible AC"}
762		require.NoError(t, store.Create(only))
763	
764		executorCalls := 0
765		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
766			Store: store,
767			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
768				executorCalls++
769				return ExecuteBeadReport{
770					BeadID:    beadID,
771					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
772					SessionID: "sess-bound",
773					ResultRev: "11111111",
774				}, nil
775			}),
776			Reviewer: makeReviewer(VerdictRequestChanges, "### Verdict: REQUEST_CHANGES\n\nStill failing."),
777		}
778	
779		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
780		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
781		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
782			// no Once flag: drain the queue fully within this run
783		})
784		require.NoError(t, err)
785	
786		// The executor should have been called exactly once; the attempted map
787		// prevents revisiting while the bead remains in_progress after review.
788		assert.Equal(t, 1, executorCalls, "bead should be attempted exactly once within a single worker.Run call")
789		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Successes)
790		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Failures, "pre-close review block counts as a failure in this run")
791	
792		// Bead remains claimed/in_progress after the blocked review.
793		got, err := store.Get(only.ID)
794		require.NoError(t, err)
795		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusInProgress, got.Status)
796	}

823	// TestGitShowExcludesEvidenceNoiseFromReviewDiff is the regression for
824	// ddx-39e27896. A prior attempt that tracked a multi-thousand-line
825	// session log (.ddx/executions/<attempt>/embedded/agent-*.jsonl) in git
826	// history would cause DefaultBeadReviewer.gitShow to emit a <diff>
827	// section sized O(session log), pushing retry prompts past 2M tokens
828	// and crashing every provider with n_keep > n_ctx.
829	//
830	// This test creates a synthetic repo matching the failure scenario —
831	// a commit that adds a 10k-line embedded session log — then runs
832	// gitShow and asserts the output excludes the embedded file content
833	// and stays bounded.
834	func TestGitShowExcludesEvidenceNoiseFromReviewDiff(t *testing.T) {
835		root := t.TempDir()
836		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
837		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
838		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
839	
840		// Seed commit so we have a base rev.
841		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(root, "README.md"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
842			t.Fatalf("write seed: %v", err)
843		}
844		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", "README.md")
845		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "seed")
846	
847		// Synthetic evidence commit that adds a multi-thousand-line session log
848		// PLUS a legitimate implementation change. The fix must exclude the
849		// session log from the diff while keeping the implementation change.
850		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx", "executions", "20260417T000000-testattempt", "embedded")
851		if err := os.MkdirAll(evidenceDir, 0o755); err != nil {
852			t.Fatalf("mkdir evidence: %v", err)
853		}
854		var bigLog strings.Builder
855		for i := 0; i < 10000; i++ {
856			fmt.Fprintf(&bigLog, "{\"seq\":%d,\"event\":\"tool_call\",\"payload\":\"lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur adipiscing elit\"}\n", i)
857		}
858		sessionLogPath := filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "agent-123.jsonl")
859		if err := os.WriteFile(sessionLogPath, []byte(bigLog.String()), 0o644); err != nil {
860			t.Fatalf("write session log: %v", err)
861		}
862	
863		// Legitimate implementation change that MUST survive in the diff.
864		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(root, "implementation.go"), []byte("package main\n\nfunc Added() {}\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
865			t.Fatalf("write implementation: %v", err)
866		}
867	
868		// Force-add the evidence (the pre-fix landEvidence behavior) plus the real change.
869		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", "-f", ".ddx/executions/")
870		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", "implementation.go")
871		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: add execution evidence [testattempt] + impl")
872	
873		// Get the HEAD sha (the evidence commit).
874		headSha := strings.TrimSpace(runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD"))
875	
876		// Call the gitShow method with the fix in place.
877		reviewer := &DefaultBeadReviewer{ProjectRoot: root}
878		out, err := reviewer.gitShow(headSha)
879		if err != nil {
880			t.Fatalf("gitShow: %v", err)
881		}
882	
883		// Must NOT include the session log content.
884		if strings.Contains(out, "lorem ipsum dolor sit amet") {
885			t.Errorf("gitShow output includes embedded session log content (pathspec exclusion not applied)")
886		}
887	
888		// Must include the legitimate implementation change.
889		if !strings.Contains(out, "implementation.go") {
890			t.Errorf("gitShow output missing the legitimate implementation file (pathspec too aggressive)")
891		}
892		if !strings.Contains(out, "func Added()") {
893			t.Errorf("gitShow output missing the implementation change body")
894		}
895	
896		// Must be bounded in size. The raw session log was ~1MB; fixed diff
897		// should be under 50KB easily (seed + impl.go + evidence metadata).
898		if len(out) > 150_000 {
899			t.Errorf("gitShow output size %d exceeds 150KB budget — pathspec exclusion did not bound the diff", len(out))
900		}
901	}

3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"fmt"
7		"strings"
8		"sync/atomic"
9		"testing"
10		"time"
11	
12		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/evidence"
15		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
16		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
17	)
18	
19	// failingReviewer always returns the given class as a typed review-error.
20	func failingReviewer(class string) beadReviewerFunc {
21		return beadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, resultRev string, _ ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
22			return &ReviewResult{
23					Verdict:   VerdictBlock,
24					Error:     class,
25					ResultRev: resultRev,
26				}, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w", class,
27					errors.New("simulated failure"))
28		})
29	}
30	
31	// seedReviewErrorEvents pre-populates a bead with N existing review-error
32	// events scoped to the given result_rev, simulating prior failed reviewer
33	// attempts. Used to drive the retry counter to its threshold without running
34	// the loop N-1 extra times.
35	func seedReviewErrorEvents(t *testing.T, store *bead.Store, beadID, resultRev, class string, n int) {
36		t.Helper()
37		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
38			require.NoError(t, store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
39				Kind:      "review-error",
40				Summary:   class,
41				Body:      ReviewErrorEventBody(class, i+1, resultRev, "prior failure"),
42				Actor:     "worker",
43				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
44				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
45			}))
46		}
47	}
48	
49	// TestBoundedReviewRetry_NthFailureEmitsManualRequired covers FEAT-022 §14
50	// case (a): after N (default 3) review-error events on the same result_rev,
51	// the loop emits a terminal `review-manual-required` event and parks the
52	// bead so a subsequent execute-loop iteration does NOT re-execute primary.
53	func TestBoundedReviewRetry_NthFailureEmitsManualRequired(t *testing.T) {
54		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
55		const resultRev = "deadbeef"
56	
57		// Seed N-1 prior failures (default N=3 → 2 prior events). The loop's
58		// reviewer attempt becomes the 3rd, which must trip the terminal event.
59		seedReviewErrorEvents(t, store, first.ID, resultRev, evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty, 2)
60	
61		var execCount atomic.Int32
62		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
63			Store: store,
64			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
65				if beadID == first.ID {
66					execCount.Add(1)
67				}
68				return ExecuteBeadReport{
69					BeadID:    beadID,
70					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
71					SessionID: "sess-r1",
72					ResultRev: resultRev,
73				}, nil
74			}),
75			Reviewer: failingReviewer(evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty),
76		}
77	
78		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
79		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
80		_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})
81		require.NoError(t, err)
82		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), execCount.Load(), "primary should run exactly once on this iteration")
83	
84		events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
85		require.NoError(t, err)
86	
87		var manual *bead.BeadEvent
88		for i := range events {
89			if events[i].Kind == "review-manual-required" {
90				manual = &events[i]
91				break
92			}
93		}
94		require.NotNil(t, manual, "Nth review failure must emit a terminal review-manual-required event")
95		assert.Contains(t, manual.Body, "failure_class="+evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
96			"manual-required body must include failure_class for triage")
97		assert.Contains(t, manual.Body, "attempt_count=3",
98			"manual-required body must include attempt_count for triage")
99		assert.Contains(t, manual.Body, "result_rev="+resultRev,
100			"manual-required body must include the blocked result_rev for triage")
101	
102		// §13 invariant: bead must NOT be closed by reviewer-failure escalation.
103		got, err := store.Get(first.ID)
104		require.NoError(t, err)
105		assert.NotEqual(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status,
106			"reviewer failure (even at terminal escalation) must never close the bead")
107	
108		// Subsequent execute-loop iteration must NOT re-execute primary. The
109		// bead is parked via SetExecutionCooldown so ReadyExecution skips it.
110		_, err = worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})
111		require.NoError(t, err)
112		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), execCount.Load(),
113			"after review-manual-required the executor must not be invoked again")
114	}
115	
116	// TestBoundedReviewRetry_FreshResultRevResetsCounter covers FEAT-022 §14
117	// case (b): a new result_rev is a fresh review scope; the counter starts
118	// from zero even when the prior result_rev exhausted its budget.
119	func TestBoundedReviewRetry_FreshResultRevResetsCounter(t *testing.T) {
120		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
121		const oldRev = "0000aaaa"
122		const newRev = "1111bbbb"
123	
124		// The previous result_rev exhausted its budget — N review-error events
125		// plus a review-manual-required event are present.
126		seedReviewErrorEvents(t, store, first.ID, oldRev, evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport, 3)
127		require.NoError(t, store.AppendEvent(first.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
128			Kind:    "review-manual-required",
129			Summary: evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport,
130			Body:    ReviewErrorEventBody(evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport, 3, oldRev, "exhausted"),
131			Actor:   "worker",
132			Source:  "ddx agent execute-loop",
133		}))
134	
135		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
136			Store: store,
137			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
138				return ExecuteBeadReport{
139					BeadID:    beadID,
140					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
141					SessionID: "sess-fresh",
142					ResultRev: newRev,
143				}, nil
144			}),
145			Reviewer: failingReviewer(evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport),
146		}
147	
148		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
149		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
150		_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})
151		require.NoError(t, err)
152	
153		events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
154		require.NoError(t, err)
155	
156		// New scope: a single review-error event for the new result_rev with
157		// attempt_count=1. NO additional review-manual-required event (the
158		// original belongs to the old scope and must not be retriggered).
159		var freshErr *bead.BeadEvent
160		manualForNewRev := 0
161		for i := range events {
162			ev := events[i]
163			if ev.Kind == "review-error" && strings.Contains(ev.Body, "result_rev="+newRev) {
164				freshErr = &events[i]
165			}
166			if ev.Kind == "review-manual-required" && strings.Contains(ev.Body, "result_rev="+newRev) {
167				manualForNewRev++
168			}
169		}
170		require.NotNil(t, freshErr, "expected a fresh review-error event scoped to the new result_rev")
171		assert.Contains(t, freshErr.Body, "attempt_count=1",
172			"a new result_rev must reset the retry counter to 1")
173		assert.Equal(t, 0, manualForNewRev,
174			"a single failure under a fresh result_rev must not emit review-manual-required")
175	}
176	
177	// TestBoundedReviewRetry_NoResultRevDoesNotConsumeBudget covers FEAT-022 §14
178	// case (c): iterations that do NOT produce a committed result_rev — execution
179	// failures and --no-merge runs (PreserveRef set, no merged ResultRev) — do
180	// not invoke the reviewer and therefore do not consume the retry budget.
181	func TestBoundedReviewRetry_NoResultRevDoesNotConsumeBudget(t *testing.T) {
182		t.Run("execution_failed", func(t *testing.T) {
183			store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
184	
185			var reviewerCalls atomic.Int32
186			reviewer := beadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _ string, _ ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
187				reviewerCalls.Add(1)
188				return nil, errors.New("reviewer should not be called for execution_failed iterations")
189			})
190	
191			worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
192				Store: store,
193				Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
194					return ExecuteBeadReport{
195						BeadID: beadID,
196						Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
197						Detail: "agent crashed",
198					}, nil
199				}),
200				Reviewer: reviewer,
201			}
202	
203			cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
204			rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
205			_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})
206			require.NoError(t, err)
207			assert.Equal(t, int32(0), reviewerCalls.Load(),
208				"reviewer must not be invoked when primary execution failed")
209	
210			events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
211			require.NoError(t, err)
212			for _, ev := range events {
213				assert.NotEqual(t, "review-error", ev.Kind,
214					"execution_failed iterations must not append review-error events")
215				assert.NotEqual(t, "review-manual-required", ev.Kind,
216					"execution_failed iterations must not consume the retry budget")
217			}
218		})
219	
220		t.Run("no_merge_preserved", func(t *testing.T) {
221			store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
222	
223			var reviewerCalls atomic.Int32
224			reviewer := beadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _ string, _ ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
225				reviewerCalls.Add(1)
226				return nil, errors.New("reviewer should not be called for --no-merge iterations")
227			})
228	
229			worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
230				Store: store,
231				Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
232					// --no-merge surrogate: a preserved iteration with no
233					// committed result_rev. The loop must not invoke the
234					// reviewer and therefore must not append review-error
235					// events that would consume the per-result_rev budget.
236					return ExecuteBeadReport{
237						BeadID:      beadID,
238						Status:      ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
239						PreserveRef: "refs/ddx/iterations/" + beadID + "/attempt-1",
240						Detail:      "preserved (--no-merge)",
241					}, nil
242				}),
243				Reviewer: reviewer,
244			}
245	
246			cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
247			rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
248			_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})
249			require.NoError(t, err)
250			assert.Equal(t, int32(0), reviewerCalls.Load(),
251				"reviewer must not be invoked for iterations without a committed result_rev")
252	
253			events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
254			require.NoError(t, err)
255			for _, ev := range events {
256				assert.NotEqual(t, "review-error", ev.Kind,
257					"--no-merge iterations must not append review-error events")
258				assert.NotEqual(t, "review-manual-required", ev.Kind,
259					"--no-merge iterations must not consume the retry budget")
260			}
261		})
262	}
263	
264	// TestBoundedReviewRetry_RespectsConfiguredOverride asserts that the
265	// ReviewMaxRetries option (sourced from .ddx/config.yaml's review_max_retries)
266	// changes the threshold at which review-manual-required is emitted.
267	func TestBoundedReviewRetry_RespectsConfiguredOverride(t *testing.T) {
268		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)

279				}, nil
280			}),

282		}

287		_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{Once: true})

318	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
319	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
320	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
321	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
322		if exitCode != 0 {
323			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
324		}
325	
326		switch outcome {
327		case "merged":
328			// A merged outcome whose reason carries the push-failure marker
329			// means the local target branch advanced but `git push` rejected the
330			// new tip (large blob, branch protection, auth, divergence, etc.).
331			// Classify these as push_failed so the loop refuses to close the
332			// bead and operators are forced to investigate.
333			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushConflictReasonPrefix) {
334				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict
335			}
336			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushFailedReasonPrefix) {
337				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed
338			}
339			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
340		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
341			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
342		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
343			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced
344		case "error":
345			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
346		case "preserved":
347			switch reason {
348			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
349				return ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict
350			case "post-run checks failed":
351				return ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
352			case RatchetPreserveReason:
353				return ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed
354			case OperatorCancelReason:
355				return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
356			default:
357				if isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason) {
358					return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
359				}
